title: crucianella glauca var. genuina
intro: Crucianella glauca var. genuina, also known as the Small Spiked Rampion, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Crucianella glauca var. genuina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits, although further research is necessary to fully understand its potential.
